relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The ancestors moved under cloud and through sea.
They were baptized into Moses through cloud and sea.
They shared spiritual food.
They drank from the spiritual rock, and the rock was Messiah.
Shared signs did not guarantee faithful desire.
Their story becomes pattern-warning for us.
Idolatry can hide inside eating, drinking, and play.
Sexual misuse can participate in death-bearing patterns.
Testing Messiah repeats wilderness danger.
Grumbling opens destructive currents.
The ancient patterns admonish the age-completion community.
Thinking you stand requires humble watchfulness.
Testing is human, and the Divine makes a way through.
Beloved ones must flee idolatry.
Discernment is invited.
Cup and bread are participation in Messiah’s blood and body.
One bread makes the many one body.
Eating sacrifice means sharing an altar.
The idol itself is nothing, yet participation still matters.
Rival sacrificial tables carry rival spiritual communion.
The Lord’s table and rival table cannot be one table.
Jealousy language warns against divided communion.
Permission is not the same as benefit or building.
Seek the other’s good, not merely your own.
Market food may be received without anxious investigation.
The earth and fullness belong to the Lord.
Hospitality may be received with gratitude.
But disclosed sacrifice calls for care toward conscience.
The other’s conscience becomes love’s concern.
Thanksgiving does not erase relational impact.
Eating, drinking, and all doing become Divine glory-space.
Do not become a stumbling block to Judeans, Greeks, or the assembly.
Paul seeks the advantage of many for their saving/healing.

contemplative_note: 1 Corinthians 10 frames communion as real participation. The question is not only what is allowed, but what table forms you and whether love builds the one body.
